# QA Issue Drafts ‚Äî Task 7 (`feat/A-B-test`)

Ready-to-paste GitHub issues for the two critical blockers. Copy each block into
**GitHub ‚Üí Issues ‚Üí New issue**. (Generated by QA because `gh` CLI is not installed
in the review environment.)

## Issue 1 ‚Äî [QA-E4] A/B result is built on a near-random classifier

- **Labels:** `bug`, `critical`, `task-7`, `model`
- **Assignee:** AIE Model

### Summary
The Task 7 A/B experiment runs and is fair/reproducible, but the underlying classifier
is near chance, so the headline conclusion ("l·ªçc stopwords hi·ªáu qu·∫£ h∆°n", ŒîF1 = +0.082)
is not trustworthy.

### Evidence
On `data/test/cleaned.csv` (2000 rows):

| Config | Accuracy | F1 (weighted) | Errors | Neg‚ÜíPos | Pos‚ÜíNeg |
|--------|---------:|--------------:|-------:|--------:|--------:|
| A (no stopword removal) | 0.518 | 0.380 | 964 (48.2%) | 954 | 10 |
| B (stopword removal) | 0.555 | 0.462 | 890 (44.5%) | 860 | 30 |

The model labels almost every negative review "Positive" ‚Äî a one-directional bias.

### Root cause
`models/classifier.py` negation logic: `score -= negative` flips a negative-vocab word to a
*positive* contribution after a negator/contrast word ("nh∆∞ng d·ªü" ‚Üí +1), and `negative` resets
every token so negation can't span. Plus `score <= 0 ‚Üí Negative` (no Neutral; ties default Negative).

### Suggested fix
- Apply a negator to a forward window of sentiment words; invert positive‚Üínegative only (never turn an already-negative word positive); remove the sign-flip on negative-vocab words.
- Consider a Neutral band / critique-cue lexicon ("n√™n","c·∫ßn","ch∆∞a","h∆°i","thi·∫øu","√≠t").
- Re-run `experiments/run_experiment.py` afterward; accept the stopword conclusion only when accuracy is meaningfully > 0.5 and the Neg‚ÜíPos bias is gone.

### Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Test-set error rate < 25% and errors balanced across directions.
- [ ] `tests/test_classifier.py` negation cases pass (`kh√¥ng kh√¥ng t·ªët`, `kh√¥ng ch·∫≥ng t·ªët`, `d·∫°y hay nh∆∞ng d·ªü`).
- [ ] A/B re-run and `docs/Experimental_Results.md` updated with the corrected numbers.

## Issue 2 ‚Äî [QA-B5] `build_vocab` overwrites committed vocab; test suite is non-hermetic

- **Labels:** `bug`, `critical`, `test-infra`
- **Assignee:** AIE Model

### Summary
`models/build_vocab.build_vocab()` hard-codes its output to `models/vocab/{pos,neg}_vocab.json`
(relative to `__file__`), ignoring any caller path. `tests/test_build_vocab.py` calls it with a
tiny fixture, so the unit test **overwrites the committed model artifacts** with `{}` (min_freq=5
empties them on small input).

### Evidence
- Running the full suite: **10 failed** vs **4 failed** when modules run in isolation.
- After a full run, `git status` shows `models/vocab/*.json` modified and reduced to 2 bytes (`{}`).
- Every test that reads the vocab after `test_build_vocab` fails (order-dependent).
- Separately, `tests/test_build_vocab.py` has 3 failing tests: it monkeypatches `models.build_vocab.load_stopwords`, which was renamed to `load_words`.

### Suggested fix
- Add an `output_dir` parameter to `build_vocab()` (default `models/vocab/`) so tests write to `tmp_path` and never touch version-controlled files.
- Update `test_build_vocab.py` to use the new param and patch `load_words` (not `load_stopwords`).

### Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `python -m pytest tests/` is order-independent and leaves `git status` clean.
- [ ] `tests/test_build_vocab.py` passes (0 failed).
